qaIssuesAddressed.description: Base-state selectors forcing charcoal text on non-hovered dark cards, making 'VIEW RESOURCE' text invisible (charcoal-on-charcoal)
qaIssuesAddressed.rootCause: The previous fix (DQA-001) added base-state selectors `.archive.dark .card-resource .btn-tertiary` and `.archive.dark .card-policy .btn-tertiary` without any :hover or pseudo-class. These forced charcoal text color on ALL btn-tertiary elements in dark mode cards, including non-hovered cards with charcoal backgrounds.
qaIssuesAddressed.fixApplied: Removed the two base-state selectors. Kept ONLY the hover/focus/group-hover variants so that charcoal text is only applied when the card/button is in an interactive state (when background is accent-colored).

filesModified.purpose: Removed base-state selectors to fix charcoal-on-charcoal text issue in dark mode non-hovered cards

instructions: Navigate to any dark mode variation (Resources Archive - Full Content or Policies Archive - Full Content)
View non-hovered resource/policy cards - 'VIEW RESOURCE' text should be visible (light/off-white color on charcoal background)
Hover over a card - card background transitions to accent (neon-green)
While card is in hover state, the 'VIEW RESOURCE' text should turn charcoal (dark) for contrast against accent background
Move cursor directly over the 'VIEW RESOURCE' text - text should remain charcoal (not fade to cream/off-white)
Move cursor away from card - text should return to light/off-white color
Verify the behavior works correctly for both resource and policy cards
Test in all three browsers at all breakpoints

technicalDetails.beforeFix: Base-state selectors applied charcoal color to btn-tertiary in ALL states (hovered and non-hovered), causing invisible text on non-hovered charcoal cards
technicalDetails.afterFix: Only hover/focus/group-hover selectors apply charcoal color, so btn-tertiary inherits its default light color on non-hovered cards (from btn-tertiary component or dark mode defaults)

colorFlow.nonHoveredCard: btn-tertiary uses default component styling (light/off-white in dark mode) - VISIBLE on charcoal background
colorFlow.hoveredCard: btn-tertiary forced to charcoal via CSS override - VISIBLE on accent background
colorFlow.directButtonHover: btn-tertiary forced to charcoal via :hover selector - VISIBLE on accent background (card is also hovered due to group)

technicalDetails.specificity: The :hover/:focus/:group-hover selectors have sufficient specificity to override component defaults when in interactive states, but don't affect the base non-interactive state

notes: This fix corrects the regression introduced in the previous DQA-001 fix

The solution balances two constraints: (1) btn-tertiary must be visible on non-hovered dark cards (light text on charcoal), and (2) btn-tertiary must be visible on hovered cards (charcoal text on accent)
By removing base-state selectors and keeping only interactive-state selectors, we achieve the correct behavior
Light mode is unaffected - it already had correct base-state selectors because white cards need charcoal text in the base state
